# UX Data Performance API Security Checklist: Abnormal Demand Surge Notifications

**Purpose**: Validate the quality of requirements across UX, data, performance, API, and security concerns for UC-11 before implementation work begins.
**Created**: 2026-03-13
**Feature**: [spec.md](/Users/sahmed/Documents/311-forecast-system/specs/011-abnormal-demand-surge-notifications/spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are the operator-facing review needs defined beyond receiving a surge alert, including what details must be visible for confirmed, suppressed, filtered, and failed outcomes? [Completeness, Spec §User Story 1, Spec §User Story 3, Plan §Implementation Notes]
- [x] CHK002 Are requirements defined for how daily versus weekly forecast products are chosen when comparing newly ingested actual demand against the active P50 forecast for the same scope? [Completeness, Spec §FR-002, Plan §Technical Context, Data Model §SurgeDetectionConfiguration]
- [x] CHK003 Are the required attributes of a surge-detection configuration fully specified, including active or inactive status, effective dates, baseline-window parameters, and channel ownership assumptions? [Completeness, Spec §Key Entities, Plan §Data Model Direction, Data Model §SurgeDetectionConfiguration]
- [x] CHK004 Are requirements defined for all API consumers that may trigger replay evaluations or read surge events, not just the operational manager persona receiving notifications? [Completeness, Spec §Assumptions, Plan §API Contract Direction, Contract §paths]
- [x] CHK005 Does the specification define what performance expectations apply to non-success outcomes such as filtered candidates, suppressed active surges, detector failures, and retry or manual-review delivery paths? [Gap, Spec §Success Criteria, Plan §Performance Goals]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK006 Is “respond quickly to a real surge even when it was not fully forecasted” translated into observable information and timing requirements rather than left as an outcome-only statement? [Clarity, Spec §User Story 1]
- [x] CHK007 Is “newly ingested actual demand” unambiguous about which ingestion slice, aggregation window, and canonical scope must be compared to the active forecast bucket? [Clarity, Spec §FR-001, Spec §FR-002, Plan §Implementation Notes]
- [x] CHK008 Is “configured notification channel or channels” precise about supported channel types, delivery ordering, and fallback semantics for surge alerts? [Clarity, Spec §FR-009, Data Model §SurgeNotificationChannelAttempt]
- [x] CHK009 Is “trigger surge detection immediately after each successful UC-01 ingestion run completes” defined clearly enough to distinguish automatic ingestion completion from protected manual replay behavior? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-001, Plan §API Contract Direction, Contract §POST /api/v1/surge-alerts/evaluations]
- [x] CHK010 Is the distinction between detector-stage candidate detection, dual-threshold confirmation, and active-surge suppression clear enough to avoid conflicting interpretations during implementation and review? [Clarity, Spec §FR-003 through §FR-007, Plan §Implementation Notes, Data Model §Confirmation Outcome Vocabulary]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK011 Are the duplicate-suppression requirements consistent between the user stories, edge cases, functional requirements, and surge-state design direction? [Consistency, Spec §User Story 1, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-019 through §FR-021, Plan §Implementation Notes, Data Model §SurgeState]
- [x] CHK012 Are delivery-success rules consistent between the spec’s successful-delivery wording and the plan or data-model distinction among `delivered`, `partial_delivery`, `retry_pending`, and `manual_review_required`? [Consistency, Spec §FR-010, Spec §FR-014, Plan §Implementation Notes, Data Model §Delivery Status Vocabulary]
- [x] CHK013 Are assumptions about externally managed notification destinations and retry or manual-review workflows consistent with the plan’s intentionally narrow API surface? [Consistency, Spec §Assumptions, Plan §Phase 0 Research Decisions, Plan §API Contract Direction]
- [x] CHK014 Are category-only and category-plus-geography surge requirements consistent with the data model’s optional geography fields and the requirement that geography remain optional in payloads and records? [Consistency, Spec §FR-018, Spec §Key Entities, Data Model §SurgeCandidate, Data Model §SurgeNotificationEvent]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK015 Can the success criteria objectively verify UX value for operational managers, or do they only measure event creation, suppression, and delivery persistence in backend records? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §Success Criteria]
- [x] CHK016 Are performance targets measurable for every critical path, including ingestion-triggered evaluation completion and surge-event review retrieval, rather than only successful notification delivery? [Measurability, Spec §SC-001 through §SC-005, Plan §Performance Goals, Plan §API Contract Direction]
- [x] CHK017 Can the requirement that failure and follow-up cases remain traceable be objectively verified without a more explicit definition of required traceability fields and operator-visible identifiers? [Measurability, Spec §FR-016, Spec §FR-017, Spec §SC-004, Data Model §SurgeNotificationEvent]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK018 Are primary UX scenarios defined for both immediate surge notification receipt and later operational review of confirmed alerts, filtered candidates, suppressed duplicates, and delivery failures? [Coverage, Spec §User Stories, Plan §Implementation Notes, Plan §API Contract Direction]
- [x] CHK019 Are alternate data scenarios specified for cases where the rolling residual baseline is unavailable, insufficient, or incompatible with the forecast product or scope? [Coverage, Gap, Spec §Assumptions, Data Model §SurgeCandidate, Data Model §SurgeDetectionConfiguration]
- [x] CHK020 Are recovery requirements defined for what should happen after a delivery failure is retried or manually reviewed, or is that lifecycle intentionally out of scope after the event enters follow-up state? [Coverage, Recovery Flow, Spec §FR-014, Spec §Assumptions]
- [x] CHK021 Are API error and authorization scenarios covered by written requirements for invalid replay requests, unauthorized event access, and missing surge-notification identifiers? [Coverage, Gap, Plan §API Contract Direction, Contract §paths]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK022 Are boundary-case requirements defined for forecast scopes where `forecast_p50_value` is zero or near zero, making percent-above-forecast ambiguous or unstable? [Edge Case, Gap, Spec §FR-005, Data Model §SurgeCandidate]
- [x] CHK023 Are data and concurrency requirements defined for overlapping ingestion completions or replay requests that evaluate the same scope while surge state is changing? [Edge Case, Gap, Spec §FR-001, Spec §FR-019 through §FR-021, Data Model §SurgeState]
- [x] CHK024 Are edge cases specified for active configurations with no usable channels because of configuration drift, despite the assumption that notification destinations exist outside this feature? [Edge Case, Assumption, Spec §Assumptions, Data Model §SurgeDetectionConfiguration]
- [x] CHK025 Are API pagination, result-size, and filtering boundaries defined for surge-event review under larger operational histories and repeated replay usage? [Edge Case, Gap, Plan §API Contract Direction, Contract §GET /api/v1/surge-alerts/events]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [x] CHK026 Are UX requirements defined for how filtered candidates, suppressed active surges, detector failures, and delivery follow-up states should be communicated to operational users if surfaced in the product? [Non-Functional, UX, Gap, Spec §User Story 2, Spec §User Story 3, Plan §Implementation Notes]
- [x] CHK027 Are data retention and auditability requirements specified for surge configurations, evaluation runs, candidates, confirmation outcomes, surge-state changes, notification events, and channel attempts? [Non-Functional, Data, Gap, Spec §FR-015 through §FR-017, Plan §Storage]
- [x] CHK028 Are performance requirements quantified for API read paths and replay-trigger acceptance, not just for ingestion-triggered confirmation timing? [Non-Functional, Performance, Gap, Plan §Performance Goals, Plan §API Contract Direction]
- [x] CHK029 Are security requirements specific about role boundaries between recipients of surge notifications and administrators or operators allowed to trigger replays and inspect detailed event histories? [Non-Functional, Security, Plan §Constitution Check, Plan §API Contract Direction, Contract §security]
- [x] CHK030 Are availability and operational-safety requirements defined for how surge-event review behaves when notification providers are degraded but stored surge outcomes remain queryable? [Non-Functional, Reliability, Gap, Plan §Constitution Check, Spec §User Story 3]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK031 Are upstream dependencies on successful UC-01 ingestion, active LightGBM forecast lineage, and rolling residual baseline availability explicitly documented as requirement constraints rather than implicit implementation assumptions? [Dependencies, Spec §Assumptions, Plan §Constitution Check, Data Model §Overview]
- [x] CHK032 Is the assumption that retry execution and manual-review workflows exist elsewhere validated by linked requirements, or should the spec state the minimum follow-up contract that UC-11 depends on? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions, Spec §FR-014]
- [x] CHK033 Are external notification-provider dependency requirements defined for timeout handling, provider references, and failure classification vocabulary at the channel-attempt level? [Dependency, Gap, Data Model §SurgeNotificationChannelAttempt, Contract §NotificationChannelAttempt]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [x] CHK034 Is there any conflict between the spec’s “one notification when a scope first enters confirmed surge state” wording and the plan’s `suppressed_active_surge` outcome where later confirmed evaluations intentionally create no new event? [Conflict, Spec §FR-019 through §FR-021, Plan §Implementation Notes, Data Model §Confirmation Outcome Vocabulary]
- [x] CHK035 Is “one surge notification event with multiple channel-attempt records” established clearly enough in the requirements, or does it currently depend on plan and data-model decisions more than spec language? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-009, Spec §FR-010, Data Model §SurgeNotificationEvent, Data Model §SurgeNotificationChannelAttempt]
- [x] CHK036 Are the terms “surge candidate,” “confirmation outcome,” “active surge state,” and “follow-up state” used consistently enough across spec, plan, data model, and API contract to support one canonical data and API vocabulary? [Consistency, Spec §Key Entities, Plan §Data Model Direction, Data Model §Confirmation Outcome Vocabulary, Contract §components.schemas]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline.
- This checklist is intentionally lightweight and author-oriented, with equal coverage across UX, data, performance, API, and security requirement quality.
